description: AI Daily Coach (Phase 9) — Review & Verify. The intelligence layer that ties all WellTrack modules together. Turns raw health data into a personalised daily plan through a morning check-in flow. Extend and fix — do not rewrite from scratch.

userStories.title: Morning check-in screen renders and saves
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Morning check-in screen accessible from dashboard and morning notification deep link
Check-in has exactly 4 questions: feeling (5 options), sleep (auto-filled from Health Connect, overridable), injuries (optional free text), schedule (3 options)
Entire check-in completable in under 30 seconds on device
Responses saved to wt_daily_checkins table in Supabase with correct user_id and date
Check-in marked complete for today — reopening shows today's plan, not the form again
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Files to audit first: lib/features/daily_coach/. Check-in data table: wt_daily_checkins. Do not rewrite from scratch. Extend and fix.

userStories.title: Sleep auto-fills from Health Connect
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Sleep field reads last night's sleep duration from wt_health_metrics (source: health_connect)
If no Health Connect sleep data exists, field defaults to empty with placeholder 'How long did you sleep?'
User can override the auto-filled value by tapping the field
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. Connect logic to existing health_repository_impl.dart or daily check-in UI.

userStories.title: Prescription engine generates correct plan type
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
prescription_engine.dart reads from real wt_daily_checkins and wt_health_metrics — no stub data
Score 80–100: plan_type = push, workout = progressive overload, calorie_adjustment = 0%
Score 60–79: plan_type = normal, workout = standard, calorie_adjustment = 0%
Score 40–59: plan_type = easy, workout = light/active recovery, calorie_adjustment = -10%
Score 0–39: plan_type = rest, workout = mobility/walk only, calorie_adjustment = sleep-focused
Feeling = 'Unwell': overrides score, sets plan_type = rest regardless
Feeling = 'Sore': reduces workout volume by 20% from standard plan
Schedule = 'Busy': swaps to 30-minute workout variant
Plan output stored in wt_daily_plans with date, user_id, plan_type, workout_id, calorie_target
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first: lib/features/daily_coach/data/prescription_engine.dart. Prescription logic lives in prescription_engine.dart as pure if/else — no AI calls in this file. If wt_daily_plans table does not exist, create migration to add it.

userStories.title: Today's Plan screen shows personalised day
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Today's Plan screen shows: workout card, meals card, steps ring, focus tip, bedtime time
Workout card shows session name, estimated duration, muscle groups — tapping opens live workout session
If plan_type = rest, workout card shows rest message and mobility suggestion instead
Meals card shows breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack names with calorie totals — tapping opens meal detail
Steps ring shows current steps vs daily target with % complete
Bedtime calculated as: device wake time + 7 hours back from 11 PM (e.g. wake 6 AM = bedtime 11 PM)
Focus tip is a single sentence from AI orchestrator explaining today's plan rationale
Screen refreshes if check-in data changes
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. Today's Plan screen is the default landing screen after check-in is complete.

userStories.title: AI narrates the plan, does not decide it
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
prescription_engine.dart produces plan output using if/else logic only — zero AI calls
ai_orchestrator_service.dart called after prescription is calculated, receives plan as input
AI prompt instructs model to explain WHY in 1–2 sentences, not what to do
AI response labelled 'WellTrack insight' in UI — never presented as a command
If AI call fails, focus tip shows a hardcoded fallback message — screen does not break
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. AI call goes through lib/shared/core/ai/ai_orchestrator_service.dart — never direct from screen. AI called once per day per user for the focus tip — result cached in wt_daily_plans.focus_tip.

userStories.title: Scenario handling for tired, behind on steps, weight stalling
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Tired scenario (feeling = Tired OR sleep < 6 hrs): workout volume reduced 20%, extra carb note added to breakfast
Behind on steps scenario (time > 3 PM AND steps < 40% of daily target): nudge card shown on dashboard 'A 30-min walk puts you on track'
Weight stalling scenario (no weight change for 14+ days AND goal = weight loss): suggestion card shown 'Consider reducing rest-day calories by 100–200kcal'
Scenario cards are dismissable — dismissal stored, card does not reappear same day
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. Check daily_coach scenario widgets and states.

userStories.title: Weekly check-in (Sunday) for sensitive metrics
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Weekly check-in prompt appears on Sunday mornings only (replaces or follows daily check-in)
Contains single question: erection quality slider 1–10
Slider value saved to wt_daily_checkins.erection_quality_weekly (integer, 1–10)
Field marked is_sensitive = true in schema — never included in AI context unless user has explicitly opted in via consent toggle in settings
Data never visible in any non-encrypted log or debug output
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. Does wt_daily_checkins already have erection_quality_weekly? If not, migration needed.

userStories.title: Morning erection Y/N logged in daily check-in
acceptanceCriteria: Read the existing file first
Daily check-in includes Y/N toggle for morning erection (shown after sleep question)
Response saved to wt_daily_checkins.morning_erection (boolean)
Field marked is_sensitive = true — never sent to AI without explicit consent
Toggle is optional — skipping it does not block check-in completion
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: Read the existing file first. Does wt_daily_checkins already have morning_erection (boolean)? If not, migration needed.
